summary: See clues file for full details. 3 tossups, 8 bonuses found.

works.name: General / Key Works
works.description: Analysis from 3 tossups and 8 bonuses. See clues file for detailed breakdown.

clues.clue: This man operated a shopfront also known as the Ray Gun Manufacturing Company, and exhibited his sculptures and found objects in the Mouse Museum.
examples: This man operated a shopfront also known as the Ray Gun Manufacturing Company, and exhibited his sculptures and found objects in the Mouse Museum.

clues.clue: This man made a weathering steel sculpture for the en-trance to SEPTA's City Hall Station which embedded the number "76" into a silvery spring.
examples: This man made a weathering steel sculpture for the en-trance to SEPTA's City Hall Station which embedded the number "76" into a silvery spring.

clues.clue: For the Walker Art Center in Minneapolis, he designed a piece with his partner Coosje van Bruggen which spans a linden seed-shaped pond.
examples: For the Walker Art Center in Minneapolis, he designed a piece with his partner Coosje van Bruggen which spans a linden seed-shaped pond.

clues.clue: Another piece of his, which was created with cardboard ice-cream cartons, is topped with a foam slice of pickle.
examples: Another piece of his, which was created with cardboard ice-cream cartons, is topped with a foam slice of pickle.

clues.clue: This artist's bookplate features a spilled ink-bottle splashing across a notebook page.
examples: This artist's bookplate features a spilled ink-bottle splashing across a notebook page.

clues.clue: This artist was inspired by a Native-American headdress in a Frederic Remington painting and an aerial lawn photograph reminiscent of a tennis court to create a set of four pieces for the sculpture garden at Nelson-Atkins Museum in Kansas City, which depicts large white feathers that must be repainted every year.
examples: This artist was inspired by a Native-American headdress in a Frederic Remington painting and an aerial lawn photograph reminiscent of a tennis court to create a set of four pieces for the sculpture garden at Nelson-Atkins Museum in Kansas City, which depicts large white feathers that must be repainted every year.

clues.clue: He once created a set of furry ice cream bars and originally included an infatable balloon at the top of a work that was initially installed on Beinecke Plaza.
examples: He once created a set of furry ice cream bars and originally included an infatable balloon at the top of a work that was initially installed on Beinecke Plaza.

clues.clue: This sculptor of the Shuttlecock series created a work in which blue bristles fail above a red wheel, with his longtime collaborator Coosje van Bruggen, and assembled an object in the Morse College courtyard at Yale, consisting of a tank-shaped base topped by the title red-tipped, vertical tube.
examples: This sculptor of the Shuttlecock series created a work in which blue bristles fail above a red wheel, with his longtime collaborator Coosje van Bruggen, and assembled an object in the Morse College courtyard at Yale, consisting of a tank-shaped base topped by the title red-tipped, vertical tube.

clues.clue: This artist's sculptures include Typewriter Eraser, Scale X and Lipstick Ascending on Caterpillar Tracks.
examples: This artist's sculptures include Typewriter Eraser, Scale X and Lipstick Ascending on Caterpillar Tracks.

clues.clue: In a collaborative show with Jim Dine, this man included his newspaper and wire based "Empire" Ray Gun.
examples: In a collaborative show with Jim Dine, this man included his newspaper and wire based "Empire" Ray Gun.

clues.clue: In 1986 this artist put on The Course of the Knife in Venice's Arsenale.
examples: In 1986 this artist put on The Course of the Knife in Venice's Arsenale.

clues.clue: That show may be considered an evolution of a style of non-verbal artistic performances this artist referred to as "Happenings." Other works of this man include one built on top of a building in Cologne, and a piece that in 1994 dangled from a railing in the Guggenheim.
examples: That show may be considered an evolution of a style of non-verbal artistic performances this artist referred to as "Happenings." Other works of this man include one built on top of a building in Cologne, and a piece that in 1994 dangled from a railing in the Guggenheim.
